title: Offload Architecture Tour
description: A guided walkthrough of Offload, a parallel test runner written in Rust with pluggable execution providers and test frameworks.

steps.title: Offload at a Glance
steps.description: Offload is a flexible parallel test runner by Imbue AI. It fans out test execution across multiple sandboxes (local processes or remote Modal environments) with automatic retry, flaky test detection, and JUnit XML reporting. The codebase follows a layered architecture: CLI entry point, configuration, providers, frameworks, orchestrator, and reporting.

steps.title: CLI Entry Point
steps.description: main.rs is the CLI entry point using clap for argument parsing. It handles subcommands: run (execute tests), collect (discover tests), validate (check config), init (generate config), and logs (view results). The lib.rs module re-exports the public API surface.

steps.title: Configuration Layer
steps.description: The config module parses offload.toml files using TOML deserialization with environment variable expansion (${VAR} and ${VAR:-default} syntax). config.rs handles loading and validation, while config/schema.rs defines the typed configuration structs: OffloadConfig, ProviderConfig, FrameworkConfig, GroupConfig, and ReportConfig.

steps.title: Execution Providers
steps.description: Providers abstract sandbox lifecycle management. The provider trait defines prepare, create, exec, destroy, and download operations. Three implementations exist: Local (child processes), Default (custom shell commands with placeholder substitution), and Modal (simplified Modal sandbox integration). The connector module handles the low-level process spawning shared across providers.

steps.title: Test Frameworks
steps.description: Frameworks handle test discovery and command generation. Each framework implements discover (list test IDs) and build_command (construct execution commands). Pytest runs --collect-only for discovery, Cargo uses nextest list, Vitest uses --reporter=json, and Default uses custom shell commands. The framework trait also defines test_id_format for matching discovered IDs to JUnit XML results.

steps.title: Orchestrator: The Engine
steps.description: The orchestrator is the heart of Offload. It coordinates the entire test run: scheduler.rs partitions tests into batches using LPT scheduling (or round-robin fallback), pool.rs manages a pool of sandboxes, spawn.rs handles concurrent batch execution across sandboxes, and runner.rs ties it all together -- driving the discover-schedule-execute-collect loop with retry logic and fail-fast support.

steps.title: Reporting & Tracing
steps.description: The report module handles JUnit XML parsing and generation. After each batch, results are matched back to discovered test IDs using test_id_format. The trace module emits Chrome Trace Event JSON (Perfetto-compatible) for visualizing execution timelines -- showing local phases, orchestrator scheduling, and per-sandbox batch execution. The bundled module manages extraction of embedded scripts (like modal_sandbox.py) from the binary.

steps.title: The Full Picture
steps.description: Offload's architecture flows top-down: CLI parses args, config loads and validates the TOML file, the orchestrator discovers tests via the framework, schedules batches, creates sandboxes via the provider, executes tests, collects JUnit results, handles retries, and produces a final report. The examples/ directory contains sample Dockerfiles and test suites for integration testing Offload itself.
